Let me give you a simple example. I own a gasoline truck. Because I ride a motorcycle and my wife is at home full time, I hardly drive it. Later, I gave it to my brother-in-law, and the parking space rented in the community was not renewed.
My wife is pregnant this year. Considering that she will pick up and drop off children in the future, she plans to buy a smaller car. After much deliberation, she decided to buy a gas car, because if she buys a tram, she has to rent a parking space + charging pile in the community, and the parking space fee is 120 a month. If you buy a gas truck, park it in a parking space outside the community. There is no charge anyway. When you use it, you can just go out and drive it in, and you don’t have to worry about not charging.
So do you understand what the problem with the tram is?

With more than 100,000 yuan, you can have the acceleration ability of about 50w of a gasoline truck. They get addicted to it and think that high horsepower is good, so they think it is easy to drive. To add some personal opinions, I only use gas and electricity, and switch between driving:
1. The tram accelerates quickly, but on urban roads, it is unsafe to start too fast at traffic lights. In fact, it does not dare to go too fast. The real advantage is smoothness.
2. The controllability is comparable to that of a low-end car, and feels about the same for home use. The advantages of trams are not obvious. As the old saying goes, there is no such thing as a car, only a person.
3. Taking Beijing as an example, the current popularity of charging piles is still not high enough, and the charging speed generally does not reach a full charge in 15 minutes. Therefore, you usually go to work, charge the car and drive away at noon, or go home and charge overnight. Charging places and work and living places need to walk several hundred meters, which is still very unfriendly in extreme high and low temperatures. For oil trucks, you don’t need to walk, just drive into the gas station. There are high-speed queues and fights over charging piles. Other vehicles (gas trucks or fully charged trams, Laotou Le) occupy charging parking spaces. Parking fees are expensive, the power system of the old community does not support it, property owners do not cooperate and it is difficult to defend rights. Parking spaces in the community are tight. There are no private parking spaces and you cannot enjoy the low electricity prices of home charging piles.
4. It is not advisable to stare at the large screen all the time when actually riding in the car, as it may cause dizziness. Vibrations and shaking are inevitable when a car is driving, and you will feel dizzy when reading a book for a long time, let alone looking at a screen. In fact, a color TV with a PAD and a car mount can be used as a makeshift replacement, so it is not a rigid necessity. The refrigerator is even worse. In other words, it would be useful if the seats were more comfortable, but this oil truck can do the same. And in terms of price alone, current gasoline vehicles of the same grade should be cheaper. The low-priced trams with high configurations mentioned by up are often new power car companies. Consumers don’t know when these car companies will become useless. To compare, BYD Song series, Titanium 3 and Cherygo 8L, Tiger 9, Geely Xingyue L, Great Wall second-generation Big Dog, H5 and Raptor, and GAC Trumpchi GS8, these cars cost around 100,000 to 150,000.
5. Trams are being updated too quickly, and infrastructure updates cannot keep up with the speed of tram updates. For a car with so-called megawatt flash charging, it is good if the actual public fast charging pile can reach 120W. There are also many 40W and 60W fast charging piles on the market. In addition, fast charging is not good for the battery and can easily produce lithium dendrites.
6. Up has mentioned one less advantage of trams. Trams are cheaper to maintain. Both the frequency and cost of maintenance are much less than that of gas trams.
In short, if you have driven a tram, you cannot speak absolutely. More than 90% of them will think that the driving experience of a tram is better!

Daily slow charging should not exceed 90%, fast charging should not exceed 80%, do not use the power below 20%, and keep the power at around 50% when parked for a long time. The lifespan will be at least twice as long as that of a full discharge.

The electric motor of the electric car completely explodes the internal combustion engine + gearbox of the oil car, while the fuel tank of the oil car explodes and kills the battery of the electric car. That's it.
The motor has a simple structure, small size, high reliability, almost no maintenance, extremely smooth, extremely fast response, low noise and long life. In contrast, the power system of the internal combustion engine + gearbox of a gasoline vehicle has all shortcomings. It has a complex structure, is difficult to adjust, requires regular maintenance, has poor ride comfort, slow response, high failure rate, and loud noise.
However, the gasoline + fuel tank of the oil truck has a simple structure, low cost, ultra-high energy density, ultra-fast energy replenishment speed, and in most cases no safety risks. In contrast, battery + BMS, in addition to naturally bringing high body rigidity, also has disadvantages, such as complex structure, high cost, heavy weight, low energy density, slow energy replenishment, and high safety risks.

As a car owner who has driven a gas car for 10 years and an electric car for one year, I would like to share my personal experience.
The biggest advantage of trams is that power is cheap and energy replenishment is cheap. Many people say that power is useless, and what is the use of a second faster traffic light in the city? I just want to ask you, don't you overtake when driving? Does the efficiency of overtaking directly affect safety? When encountering danger, is there another option to accelerate besides braking? Anyone who says power is useless is someone who has never touched a four-wheel drive or electric car. The biggest disadvantage of the tram is that it is inconvenient to recharge during holidays. You have to queue up at the service area or get off the expressway to recharge. However, recharging is much cheaper than refueling. If you have a home, the cost of using the tram is lower than that of the bus. For long distances, I charged about 500 yuan for two trips of 2,400 kilometers, which is a lot of gas. All the car owners on the Internet seem to be well-paid. If a gas station held a campaign saying that if you park your car at the gas station and wait for half an hour, you can get a 50% discount on the price of gas, the gas station will probably be crowded. Anyway, I am an ordinary person, and I can't earn half a tank of gas in half an hour [doge_金环].
Also talking about insurance, my bare electric car costs 27.2W, the first year insurance is 7500, the second year insurance is 3900, both are full insurance and three liability 300W. Owners of oil trucks who say insurance is expensive can report their own insurance prices and let us know how to increase it.
Others say that if you get motion sickness on a tram, you can just turn off the kinetic energy recovery, otherwise you will almost certainly get motion sickness. Also, it is normal to get motion sickness when you first start driving a tram under rapid acceleration. You will get used to it after a week of driving. If you don’t accelerate quickly, it will be almost the same as a gas car. My car accelerates from 100 kilometers to 100 kilometers in 4 seconds, and I feel like the 7-second tram can be used casually without getting dizzy.
There is also the issue of value preservation. In today’s market, gasoline vehicles cannot maintain their value. Of course, electric vehicles depreciate faster. A big factor is that some important warranty items are only valid for the first owner. There is also residual value. A petrol car that is more than ten years old may be sold for less than 10,000, but an electric car that is more than ten years old can definitely be sold for 10,000. You might as well go to deepseek to ask about the recycling prices of lithium iron phosphate and ternary lithium. I cannot say that the residual value of electric cars is high, but it must be higher than most people think.
Then there is the issue of battery life. I have been driving it for a year now, mainly slow charging, and there is almost no attenuation.
And in terms of maintenance, the maintenance frequency of electric cars is one year or 20,000 kilometers, whichever comes first. This is definitely cheaper than oil cars. Oil cars require 5,000-8,000 maintenance, and the single maintenance cost is also higher than that of electric cars.
Then there is the purchase tax for trams, which saves 10,000 to 20,000 yuan. It is simple and crude, and there is nothing to say. Policies for daily use of trams and trams are better, with no plate restrictions, discounted parking, and the ability to use bus lanes, etc.
Also talking about tires. My 20-inch original tires cost 1,600 per tire. On the day I picked up the car, two tires were scratched by a pothole on the road. I replaced them with 620 per tire. They have been used 2.5W kilometers per year and everything is normal. I don’t know the price of 20-inch tires for gasoline trucks. Anyone who knows can tell me.
There is also spontaneous combustion. I have never seen spontaneous combustion of oil trucks and trams in real life. I have seen spontaneous combustion of petrol trucks and trams on Douyin. The topic of spontaneous combustion of trams is more popular, and the probability of spontaneous combustion cannot be seen.

Before considering which car to buy, it all depends on your actual situation. You can get the answer by answering a few questions.
1. Are there charging conditions? If you have a garage at home or a charging pile not far away, you can ensure that you can charge it every day. Walking for more than fifteen minutes is not considered close. Beyond this distance, I worry that you will have a bad charging experience.
2. What is the approximate number of kilometers per year? Within 15,000 kilometers per year, the money-saving advantage of the tram cannot be reflected, so it is meaningless.
    If you can easily charge and walk home within fifteen minutes, then pure electricity is the best choice.
    If you have charging conditions and a lot of kilometers per year, especially on high-speed and long distances, it is best to choose a hybrid. You will not be in a situation where you need a car for an urgent trip and have no car available.
   If you don’t have charging conditions, then don’t consider the tram at all. Needless to say, if the charging pile is too far and you have to walk home for more than 15 minutes every day, you will find charging troublesome.
   The number of kilometers is small. The gas savings of electric cars compared to gasoline cars must be added up by the number of kilometers to make sense. It is meaningful to save more than 10,000 yuan in gas money per year. If the gas money saved is less than 10,000 yuan, then in exchange for such a small amount of money to have a charging environment, I think it cannot be said that it is not worth it. I can only say that it is of little significance.
Take myself as an example. I used to drive a 2.0t SUV. I drove 40,000 kilometers per year and averaged 11 oil. The oil price fluctuated for a long time at 7.5 yuan. I use public charging piles for charging, and the electricity service fee is 1.2 per kilowatt hour. With such high electricity prices, 20,000 yuan a year is enough.
